definitions: 珍馐美味；稀有而昂贵的食物或享受品

examples.text: They also made wine, but because the climate wasn’t ideal for growing grapes, it was a rare and expensive delicacy. By contrast, in Greece and Rome, where grapes grew more easily, wine was as readily available as beer was in Egypt and Mesopotamia.
examples.explanation: 这里 delicacy 指“珍贵的美食/佳品”。句子说由于当地气候不适合种葡萄，葡萄酒在当时是一种稀有且昂贵的享受品。

source.text: A brief history of alcohol - Rod Phillips
